2606.27743.title: End-to-End Dynamic Sparsity for Resource-Adaptive LLM Inference
2606.27743.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) inference is typically deployed under a static resource assumption, where models execute a fixed computational graph regardless of the runtime environment. However, real-world cloud infrastructure is inherently dynamic, characterized by fluctuating availability (e.g., spot instance preemption) and tiered Quality-of-Service requirements. In such volatile settings, static models are inflexible: they either crash under resource constraints or waste compute on redundant operations. To bridge this gap, we propose Learning to Allocate (L2A), an end-to-end framework for resource-adaptive inference. Unlike prior methods that condition only on input difficulty, we formulate inference as a constrained allocation problem conditioned on both the input and the runtime resource budget itself. We introduce lightweight, budget-conditioned and input-aware gating networks integrated into the LLM. These gates are trained via a unified objective that jointly optimizes task performance, logical consistency, and resource costs along three axes matching how real-world dynamics manifest: layer skipping for memory and depth pressure, head pruning for throughput contention, and reasoning-token reduction for latency tightening. This lets the model learn a budget-aware policy beyond input difficulty alone: it adaptively configures its computational footprint with respect to real-time resource dynamics, maximizing reasoning depth when resources permit while enforcing strict frugality when budgets tighten. A single L2A model traces the entire compute-accuracy Pareto frontier on Llama-3-8B and Qwen-3-4B: at up to 34% realized layer sparsity, it stays within 0.6% of the dense baseline on GSM8K, with the same gap holding zero-shot on out-of-distribution tasks, while every static or heuristic baseline requires a separately tuned model and still drops by 5-10% at comparable inference time.
2606.27743.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的推理通常在靜態資源假設下進行，其中模型執行固定的計算圖，無論運行環境如何。然而，現實世界的雲基礎設施本質上是動態的，特徵是可用性波動（例如，臨時實例的搶佔）和分層的服務質量要求。在這種不穩定的環境中，靜態模型缺乏靈活性：它們要麼在資源限制下崩潰，要麼在冗餘操作上浪費計算資源。為了解決這一問題，我們提出了學習分配（L2A），這是一個端到端的資源自適應推理框架。與之前僅根據輸入難度進行條件設定的方法不同，我們將推理形式化為一個約束分配問題，這個問題同時依賴於輸入和運行時資源預算。我們引入了輕量級的、預算條件和輸入感知的閘網絡，這些網絡集成到LLM中。這些閘網絡通過一個統一的目標進行訓練，該目標共同優化任務性能、邏輯一致性和資源成本，沿著三個軸線匹配現實世界動態的表現：層跳過以應對記憶和深度壓力、頭修剪以應對吞吐量競爭，以及推理標記減少以應對延遲收緊。這使得模型能夠學習超越僅僅依賴輸入難度的預算感知策略：它根據實時資源動態自適應配置其計算足跡，在資源允許的情況下最大化推理深度，同時在預算緊縮時強制執行嚴格的節約。一個單一的L2A模型在Llama-3-8B和Qwen-3-4B上追蹤整個計算-準確性Pareto邊界：在實現高達34%的層稀疏性時，它在GSM8K上保持在密集基線的0.6%以內，並且在零-shot的分佈外任務中保持相同的差距，而每個靜態或啟發式基線都需要單獨調整的模型，並且在可比較的推理時間內仍然下降5-10%。
2606.27743.author: Yuhang Chen et.al.
2606.27743.authors: Yuhang Chen, Jinhao Duan, Ruichen Zhang, Mingfu Liang, Xiaohan Wei, Yunchen Pu, Fei Tian, Chonglin Sun, Parish Aggarwal, Frank Shyu, Luke Simon, Sandeep Pandey, Tianlong Chen, Xi Liu
